_WARNING: THIS IS NOT AN FBLA RUBRIC. It is a generic, invented placeholder whose only job is to exercise the grading pipeline end to end during M1. Its criteria and point values came from nobody official. Do not grade a real competitor against it, do not ship it, and do not treat any score produced with it as meaningful. Replace it with a hand-structured rubric from the real official rating sheet before the grader is used for anything that matters. See rubrics/README.md.
title: DEV PLACEHOLDER — Generic Prepared Speech (not an official rubric)

criteria.name: Opening and hook
criteria.description: Does the speaker capture attention in the first thirty seconds and make the topic feel like it matters?

criteria.name: Organization and structure
criteria.description: Is there a clear thesis, a logical progression of sections, and audible signposting between them?

criteria.name: Evidence and support
criteria.description: Are claims backed by specific facts, figures, examples, or sources rather than assertion?

criteria.name: Delivery and fluency
criteria.description: Pace, vocal variety, and freedom from distracting disfluency. A delivery criterion, so fluency counts here.

criteria.name: Language and word choice
criteria.description: Precise, vivid, audience-appropriate language; free of filler and vagueness.

criteria.name: Closing and call to action
criteria.description: Does the speech land — a memorable close that resolves the thesis and tells the audience what to do or think?
